_comment_provenance: System parameters: Esposito et al. 2017 (A&A 601, A53), the source Bell et al. 2024 used for their fiducial fit: M*=0.688 Msun, R*=0.6506 Rsun, Rp=1.006 Rjup, b=0.689, i=82.109 deg, log g_p=3.696 (g=49.66 m/s^2). Ephemeris: Ivshina & Winn 2022 (ApJS 259, 62), P=0.813474037 d; omega=2*pi/P (tidally locked). a_planet_m is the SHALLOW-WATER SPHERE RADIUS (planet radius, 1.006 Rjup in meters), NOT the orbital semi-major axis (jaxoplanet derives the orbit from star_mass_msun + period). Phibar=4.0e6 m^2/s^2 is the Perez-Becker & Showman 2013 standard gH (T_bar=Phibar/R_d=1058 K); DPhieq=3.5e6 gives a substellar equilibrium T of ~1984 K (~T_*sqrt(R*/a)) and DPhieq/Phibar=0.875, inside the PBS13 forcing range. The Planck band wavelengths/weights are injected at run time from observations.npz (written by prepare_wasp43b_observations.py).
_comment_solver: dt=120 s and K6=5e33 (vs the SWAMPE synthetic-regime defaults 240 s / 1.24e33): the Phibar=4e6 regime has gravity waves ~3.7x faster than the synthetic Phibar=3e5 setup, so the hyperdiffusion is rescaled with the wave speed (K6 ~ U*dx^5 -> 1.24e33 * sqrt(4e6/3e5) ~ 4.5e33) and dt halved. Verified 20-day stable at all corners of the (tau_rad, tau_drag) prior box; at dt=240 the run blows up late in spin-up, and at dt=120 with the default K6 the doubly-undamped (48 h, 48 h) corner blows up. Truth-region T maps are identical to the default-K6 solution (grid-scale dissipation only).
